channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Doctor Celebrates First Car Purchase After Years of Hard Work in Australia
item.description: Dr Fayth, a female doctor based in Australia, celebrated buying her very first car, a Porsche Macan S Twin-Turbocharged, after years of grinding through the demands of medicine. The milestone was shar...

item.title: The Role of Communication in Strengthening Marital Relationships
item.description: Effective communication is vital for maintaining healthy marriages, as it fosters love, trust, and understanding between partners. Many couples overlook the importance of meaningful dialogue, but expe...

item.title: Fremantle and Sydney Swans clash in crucial AFL 2026 match
item.description: The AFL 2026 season reached a pivotal moment as Fremantle, the league leaders, host the second-placed Sydney Swans in a high-stakes encounter in Perth. The match, set against a backdrop of intense riv...

item.title: DepEd addresses surge in school violence following Tacloban shooting incident
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has reported a significant increase in school-related violence incidents across the Philippines, citing the deadly Tacloban shooting as a catalyst for the 'copycat'...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Withdraws Amid Allegations of Past Misconduct
item.description: Graham Platner, a progressive Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, suspended his campaign on July 8 after allegations surfaced regarding past misconduct. The article highlights how modern political c...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Announces Joint Civil-Military Effort to Combat Terrorism in Balochistan
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif declared that civil and military leaders have collectively decided to eradicate terrorism following recent attacks in Balochistan. During a meeting in Quetta, he emphasiz...

item.title: Senator Robin Padilla Visits Rodante Marcoleta at PNP General Hospital for Health Checkup
item.description: Senator Robin Padilla visited Senator Rodante Marcoleta at the Philippine National Police General Hospital (PNPGH) on Thursday, July 9, 2026, as Marcoleta undergoes medical tests to determine his fitn...

item.title: Christopher Nolan praises Samantha Morton's performance in 'The Odyssey' comparing it to Heath Ledger's iconic role
item.description: Christopher Nolan has publicly praised Samantha Morton’s portrayal of goddess Circe in his film 'The Odyssey', drawing a parallel to the late Heath Ledger’s legendary performance as The Joker in 'The ...

item.title: Ecuador's President Vows to Resist U.S. Intervention in Domestic Affairs
item.description: Ecuador's president has publicly stated that he will not allow the United States to intervene in the country's internal affairs, a declaration that comes amid heightened tensions between the two natio...

item.title: Al Carns backs Andy Burnham for Labour leadership amid contest
item.description: Former Defence Minister Al Carns has confirmed he will not seek the Labour leadership, endorsing Andy Burnham as the party's next leader. Carns stated that a leadership contest is 'not the best use of...

item.title: Heavy Rain Causes Waterlogging in Delhi and LPG Cylinders Swept Away in Maharashtra
item.description: Heavy rainfall continued in Delhi and the National Capital Region (NCR) on Thursday, leading to severe waterlogging and traffic disruptions. The Mehrauli-Badarpur road and Burari area in northern Delh...

item.title: Nigerian Army Soldiers Petition Tinubu Over Delay in Voluntary Discharge
item.description: A group of serving Nigerian Army personnel have petitioned President Bola Tinubu, the National Assembly, and top defense officials over alleged delays in processing voluntary discharge applications. T...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler Passes Away at 75 After Illness, Leaving Unforgettable Legacy
item.description: Bonnie Tyler, the Welsh singer known for her iconic hits like 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has passed away at 75 after a brief illness. She died on July 8, 2026, in Portugal following emergency surge...

item.title: Susan Collins' Senate Campaign Faces Challenges After Opponent's Exit
item.description: Senator Susan Collins' (R-ME) Senate re-election campaign is in turmoil after Democrat Graham Platner, her primary opponent, abruptly suspended his campaign due to credible allegations of sexual misco...

item.title: Calgary police shooting: Family of woman with mental health struggles speaks out
item.description: The family of Melanie Hambrook, a 53-year-old Edmonton woman fatally shot by Calgary police during a confrontation on Deerfoot Trail in June 2026, has shared their grief and concerns. Adam Vaananen, h...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks crawler bots with access restrictions
item.description: The article discusses News Corp Australia's measures to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Readers or subscribers encountering a block message are advised to check their browser settings, d...

item.title: Patelo and Dee Reappear at Outdoor Lounge After Months of Privacy
item.description: VJ Patelo and his wife Dee have resurfaced after months of keeping a low profile, sparking renewed interest. The couple, who gained fame from their lavish 2025 wedding in Limuru, were spotted at an up...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Visits Quetta for Law and Order Meeting

item.title: Brits Paying Extra for Overpriced Contracts: Survey Reveals 38% Feel They're Overcharged
item.description: A survey of 2,000 adults reveals 38% of Brits are stuck in contracts they believe overcharge them by £576 annually. Half of respondents noticed rising prices over time, while 26% feel they receive poo...

item.title: Two Separate Shootings Kill Seven, Including Teenagers, in Cape Town
item.description: In Cape Town, two separate shootings occurred on Wednesday night, 8 July 2026, resulting in seven fatalities and multiple injuries. The first incident took place at around 8:10pm in Tafelsig, Mitchell...
